prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a small determined robot grips the wheel of a tiny boat, choosing between two branching glowing rivers ahead while a meek scientist beside it merely holds up a crystal ball watching the same forks drift by. Style friendly like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, textbook suitable. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot steering a boat toward one of two branching rivers while a passive scientist only gazes into a crystal ball, contrasting acting to shape the future against merely predicting it.
caption: Forecasting watches the river fork; decision making grabs the wheel and picks a branch.

after_heading: 1. The Markov Property: The Future Forgets the Past Given the Present
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a calm detective stands on a single glowing footprint in the present, while behind him a long trail of older footprints crumbles into fog and dissolves, yet he confidently points forward, needing only the spot he stands on to plan his next step. Style friendly like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, textbook suitable. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A detective standing on one glowing present footprint while the trail of past footprints behind him dissolves into fog, illustrating that the future depends only on the current state.
caption: Given where you stand right now, the whole crumbling trail of history adds nothing to your next move.

after_heading: 3. The Bellman Equations: Expectation and Optimality
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a friendly robot holds a coin in one hand representing the reward right now, and with the other hand peers into a slightly smaller mirror that reflects itself peering into an even smaller mirror, each reflection a step further ahead and gently dimmed, building an infinite shrinking corridor of future selves. Style friendly like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, textbook suitable. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot holding an immediate reward coin while gazing into nested shrinking mirrors of itself, each reflection dimmer, visualizing value as immediate reward plus a discounted recursion of future value.
caption: A situation is worth what you grab now plus a faded echo of what every future self can grab, all the way down.

after_heading: 4. Policy Improvement: Acting Greedily Never Hurts
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a hiker pauses at a junction on a foggy hillside, consults a glowing height map floating in one hand, then takes the single uphill step that the map promises is highest, while a faint ghost of his old aimless path trails behind and sits lower. Style friendly like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, textbook suitable. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A hiker at a junction consulting a glowing value map and taking the one locally highest uphill step, with his old lower wandering path fading behind, depicting greedy policy improvement never lowering value.
caption: Swap each move for the one your current value map rates highest and you can only ever end up better off.

after_heading: 2. Why They Converge: The Bellman Operator Is a Contraction
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm palette: a cheerful mechanical press shaped like a friendly hand repeatedly squeezes a wobbly oversized error blob, and with each squeeze the blob shrinks toward a tiny glowing fixed dot on the floor, a faint stack of progressively smaller ghost blobs showing it has been pressed many times and is nearly gone. Style friendly like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face on the blob looking resigned, one clear visual gag, generous negative space, textbook suitable. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A friendly press repeatedly squeezing a wobbly error blob smaller and smaller toward a single glowing fixed point, with ghost stages of shrinking, showing the Bellman operator as a contraction that converges.
caption: Every sweep shrinks the gap to the true answer by a fixed fraction, so the error is squeezed relentlessly toward zero.
